div.head:
3565.1 Robert Southey to Mrs Morgan, 24 November 1820 Address: To/ Mrs Morgan/ Central School/ Baldwins Gardens
Postmarks: 2 o’Clock/ 27.No/ 1820 A.M.; [1 illegible postmark]
Seal: Trace
Endorsement: R. Southey Esqre
MS: H.R. Woudhuysen, Oxford. ALS; 3p.
Unpublished.
div.p: I anticipated the contents of your letter when I saw the seal, knowing the likelihood of such an event. Mary Anne Morgan’s husband, Southey’s old schoolfriend, John James Morgan, had just died. He had become very ill in 1819 and had been in severe financial difficulties since the mid-1810s. To you it must needs have been a severe shock, & a deep affliction; but the death of a good man must always be to his own gain, & more especially when he has been an afflicted one. Unfortunate I will not call him, however adverse his circumstances have been in later years, – because he had you for his wife. – I had known him nearly forty years, & shall always remember him with respect & regret.
Mr Kenyon is not in England, nor do I know where he is. But whenever I obtain his direction I will write to him.

I am sorry to hear that your strength fails you. You have had sore trials, sufficient indeed to shake it. But the worst is past; – & you have the comfort (above all others, & above all price) of having done your duty to the utmost.
Do not let me be uninformed of any change which may take place in your situation: but think of me always as as your sincere friend for your own sake as well as for poor Morgans.

Mrs Southey & her sisters Sara Coleridge and Mary Lovell. desire their kindest remembrances. I need not say how much they have felt for you in your troubles. Remember me kindly to your sister,Charlotte Brent (1783–after 1820). & believe me,
